source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Extend the range of sentences with more than one clause by using a wider range of conjunctions, including when, if, because and although; use conjunctions, adverbs and prepositions to express time and cause; and develop expanded noun phrases for description and specification.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack combines the linked lower-key-stage-2 expectations into purposeful expansion: add precise noun detail, connect complete ideas, and choose time or cause language without creating fragments, run-ons or overloaded sentences.

objective.child_goal: I can add useful detail and join ideas so my sentence says exactly what I mean.
objective.statement: Expand noun phrases and sentences with precise detail, clauses and time-or-cause links while preserving clear grammar and meaning.
misconceptions: Adds several adjectives without choosing details that help the reader identify or imagine the noun.
Treats every longer group of words as a complete clause and produces a fragment after a conjunction.
Joins clauses with a conjunction that does not match the intended time, cause or contrast relationship.
Adds so many phrases and clauses that the main meaning becomes unclear or punctuation is lost.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A noun with words that belong with it and tell the reader which person, place, thing or idea is meant.
vocabulary.audio_script: A noun phrase names something and can include useful detail.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A noun tile with detail tiles attached around it.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A group of words built around a verb.
vocabulary.audio_script: A clause contains a verb and adds an action, event or state.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A sentence block with a glowing verb socket.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A linking word that shows how ideas fit together.
vocabulary.audio_script: A conjunction can link ideas by time, cause, condition or contrast.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A labelled bridge between two clause blocks.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A word that begins a phrase telling where, when or how things are related.
vocabulary.audio_script: Prepositions such as beneath, after and during begin detail phrases.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A position or time arrow beginning at a preposition card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Chosen carefully so the meaning is clear and exact.
vocabulary.audio_script: Precise detail earns its place by helping the reader.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A focus lens selecting one useful detail from several.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Find the main noun and the verb in each short sentence.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Retrieve the sentence anchors needed before adding phrases and clauses.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Find who or what the sentence is about, then find the action or state.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Noun and verb tiles lock into labelled slots without relying on colour.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Expand 'the door' so a reader knows exactly which door, but keep only details that help.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Frame expansion as a meaning choice rather than a contest to make sentences longer.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Useful detail can identify, describe or explain. Extra words are not always better.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Candidate detail tiles pass through IDENTIFY, DESCRIBE and REMOVE gates.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Compare: 'The path flooded because the rain continued' and 'The path flooded although the rain continued.'
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model how conjunction choice changes the relationship between complete ideas.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Because gives a cause. Although gives an unexpected contrast. The link must match the meaning.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two clause blocks stay fixed while the relationship bridge changes label and meaning.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose one useful noun detail, add a time or cause phrase, then join a second clause if it improves the message.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate noun-phrase expansion, phrase placement and clause linking in manageable stages.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build, read, and check after each addition. Keep the main message easy to find.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A three-stage editor shows noun detail, phrase detail and optional clause link separately.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Is 'Because the gate was locked' a complete message here, or does it need another clause?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose fragments and test whether conjunction-led clauses have a complete partner.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Because introduces a reason. Ask what happened because the gate was locked.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The because-clause waits beside an empty main-clause socket.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach the companion why the clearest sentence is not always the longest one.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure editing judgement and resistance to adjective or clause overload.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Point to each added part, name its job and remove anything that does not help.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A sentence meter compares useful meaning with reading load, without scoring length.

manipulatives.purpose: Make noun detail, phrases, clauses and their meaning relationships visible during composition and revision.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners add, remove or reorder labelled chunks; test conjunction meanings; hear the sentence after each change; and compare a concise and expanded version.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Every drag action has tap, keyboard and switch-step alternatives; chunks have text labels and spoken roles; oral composition can precede typing; line focus and adjustable spacing are available; no judgement depends on colour or reading speed.

manipulatives.purpose: Connect two clause ideas with explicit time, cause, condition or contrast relationships.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners select a relationship first, then choose when, if, because, although or another suitable link and replay the complete meaning.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: A linear two-sentence mode replaces spatial linking; conjunctions include short definitions and audio examples; choices can be reduced without simplifying the target relationship.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure useful noun detail and complete-clause recognition separately, then interleave time, cause, condition and contrast links in short narrative and non-fiction contexts.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Expand a noun with details that identify, describe or specify it without redundant adjective lists.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: base noun + communicative purpose + adjective/prepositional detail choices + concise read-back
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept varied natural orderings; reject value-laden descriptions of people and avoid implying that more modifiers mean better writing.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Recognise verb-based clauses and repair conjunction-led fragments by supplying a complete partner.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: main clause or subordinate clause chunk + verb check + complete/needs-part decision + targeted repair
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use oral-natural examples and do not define every clause as a complete sentence.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose conjunctions, adverbs or prepositional phrases that express the intended relationship accurately.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two complete ideas + named relationship + link choices including when/if/because/although + meaning replay
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check that alternatives are genuinely distinct in context and allow more than one answer only when each relationship is explicitly justified.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Revise overloaded or ambiguous sentences so added detail remains grammatical, purposeful and easy to follow.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: over-expanded sentence + labelled chunks + retain/remove/reorder/link options + reader-meaning check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not reward a single house style; preserve author voice and accept a shorter sentence when it communicates better.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain phrase, clause and relationship choices through short mixed review and transfer into paragraph contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + one sentence or two-sentence context + choose useful detail/link/edit + brief reason
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep decoding demand modest, provide audio, and include both narrative and explanation writing without cultural assumptions.

body.prompt: The reader must identify the key that opens the attic. Which noun phrase is most useful?
choices: the small brass key with a star-shaped bow

interaction_route.touch: Tap a sentence chunk or numbered choice, then tap check; moving chunks by drag is optional.
interaction_route.keyboard: Tab through prompt, clause or noun-phrase chunks and choices; use arrows to review and Enter or Space to select, place or check.
interaction_route.switch_scan: Scan prompt, chunk map, numbered choices, check and retry in a fixed order with one activation per decision.
interaction_route.eye_gaze: Use large dwell-select chunk and choice targets with adjustable dwell time and a confirm step.
interaction_route.aac_oral_adult_scribed: The learner may point, use AAC, compose orally or direct an adult to select/place/transcribe the indicated answer without the adult supplying the grammatical judgement.

body.accessible_response_route: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition, pointing and adult-scribed responses provide equivalent grammar evidence; dragging, handwriting and speech are never mandatory.
body.chunking_route: Prompt and options can be revealed one sentence or labelled meaning chunk at a time with line focus, adjustable spacing and correct chunks preserved.
body.clause_map_route: Conjunction-led clause, main clause, subject/command focus and verb are shown in separate labelled text boxes; a linear text list replaces spatial mapping.
body.noun_phrase_route: Determiner, noun and useful identifying/describing detail are bracketed separately; vague adjective piles and words outside the noun phrase can be compared without colour-only cues.
body.meaning_comparison_route: Original and revised sentences remain side by side with relationship labels for cause, time, condition or contrast and a concise read-for-meaning question.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one choice, clause boundary, conjunction relationship or editing decision at a time while retaining the complete target meaning and correct work.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: the small brass key with a star-shaped bow

hints: Choose details that help someone recognise the key.
A phrase can include a useful prepositional detail.
question_variants.explanation: The size, material and star-shaped bow help the reader identify the particular key; the other choices are vague or ungrammatical.

feedback.correct: “the small brass key with a star-shaped bow” keeps the noun easy to identify and adds specific detail with a clear job instead of piling up vague praise.
feedback.repair: Find the head noun, ask what detail helps the reader identify or picture this exact noun, keep that grammatical detail and remove vague praise, repeated adjectives or words that belong outside the noun phrase.
feedback.grammar_evidence: The selected noun phrase “the small brass key with a star-shaped bow” contains a head noun plus precise identifying, describing or specifying detail; it remains one grammatical noun phrase.
feedback.misconception_support: adjective_pile: Find the head noun, ask what detail helps the reader identify or picture this exact noun, keep that grammatical detail and remove vague praise, repeated adjectives or words that belong outside the noun phrase.
feedback.strategy_support: Use HEAD NOUN → DETAIL WITH A JOB → REMOVE REPETITION → REREAD.
feedback.support_message: Chunk selection, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition and adult scribing are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct meaning and sentence chunks stay. Inspect one noun-detail, clause-boundary, conjunction or clarity clue, then retry without losing progress.

expected_answer.value: Because the gate was locked, the team used the side path.

hints: The because-clause gives a reason.
Add a clause that tells what happened.
question_variants.explanation: The repaired sentence includes the reason and a main clause explaining the result.

feedback.correct: “Because the gate was locked, the team used the side path.” repairs the fragment by pairing the conjunction-led clause with a complete main clause that tells what happened.
feedback.repair: Box the conjunction-led clause and find its verb, then ask ‘what happened?’ Add a complete main clause or command with its own verb so the whole message is complete.
feedback.grammar_evidence: The selected completion “Because the gate was locked, the team used the side path.” supplies the action, event or command needed by the opening clause, so the message is no longer a conjunction-led fragment.
feedback.misconception_support: conjunction_fragment: Box the conjunction-led clause and find its verb, then ask ‘what happened?’ Add a complete main clause or command with its own verb so the whole message is complete.
feedback.strategy_support: Use FIND VERB → ASK WHAT HAPPENED → SUPPLY COMPLETE PARTNER → REREAD WHOLE MESSAGE.
feedback.support_message: Chunk selection, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition and adult scribing are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct meaning and sentence chunks stay. Inspect one noun-detail, clause-boundary, conjunction or clarity clue, then retry without losing progress.

expected_answer.value: Although

hints: The walkers continued even with the muddy path.
Choose the word that signals contrast.
question_variants.explanation: 'Although' shows that continuing was unexpected in contrast with the muddy conditions.

feedback.correct: “Although” expresses the intended contrast relationship while keeping the linked ideas clear.
feedback.repair: Name the relationship before choosing: because gives cause, although gives contrast, if gives condition, and when/before give time. Reread both ideas with the selected link and reject a link that changes the intended meaning.
feedback.grammar_evidence: The selected answer “Although” matches contrast meaning rather than being chosen only because it sounds familiar.
feedback.misconception_support: link_by_sound: Name the relationship before choosing: because gives cause, although gives contrast, if gives condition, and when/before give time. Reread both ideas with the selected link and reject a link that changes the intended meaning.
feedback.strategy_support: Use NAME RELATIONSHIP → CHOOSE CONJUNCTION → CHECK BOTH CLAUSES → REREAD MEANING.
feedback.support_message: Chunk selection, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition and adult scribing are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct meaning and sentence chunks stay. Inspect one noun-detail, clause-boundary, conjunction or clarity clue, then retry without losing progress.

expected_answer.value: The enormous robot beside the gate beeped when it saw us.

hints: Keep the details that identify the robot and explain the timing.
Remove repeated size words and repair the clause link.
question_variants.explanation: The edit keeps useful size, place and time information while removing repetition and restoring a clear sentence structure.

feedback.correct: “The enormous robot beside the gate beeped when it saw us.” keeps the central message easy to follow, retains useful detail and removes repetition or broken clause links.
feedback.repair: Underline the core subject or command focus and verb, bracket each added phrase or clause, keep only detail with a clear job, and repair or remove any repetition or link that hides the main meaning.
feedback.grammar_evidence: The revision “The enormous robot beside the gate beeped when it saw us.” preserves the main message and useful description while reducing repetition, overload or unclear clause structure.
feedback.misconception_support: expansion_overload: Underline the core subject or command focus and verb, bracket each added phrase or clause, keep only detail with a clear job, and repair or remove any repetition or link that hides the main meaning.
feedback.strategy_support: Use FIND CORE MESSAGE → BRACKET ADDED DETAIL → REMOVE/REPAIR ONE UNCLEAR PART → REREAD FOR CLARITY.
feedback.support_message: Chunk selection, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition and adult scribing are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.
feedback.retry: Your correct meaning and sentence chunks stay. Inspect one noun-detail, clause-boundary, conjunction or clarity clue, then retry without losing progress.

expected_answer.value: The bulb stayed off because the circuit had a gap.

hints: Read every option as a complete message.
Check whether the detail or link matches the writer's purpose.
question_variants.explanation: The selected conjunction matches the intended relationship, and both linked ideas remain complete and clear.

feedback.correct: “The bulb stayed off because the circuit had a gap.” expresses the intended cause relationship while keeping the linked ideas clear.
feedback.repair: Name the relationship before choosing: because gives cause, although gives contrast, if gives condition, and when/before give time. Reread both ideas with the selected link and reject a link that changes the intended meaning.
feedback.grammar_evidence: The selected answer “The bulb stayed off because the circuit had a gap.” matches cause meaning rather than being chosen only because it sounds familiar.
feedback.misconception_support: link_by_sound: Name the relationship before choosing: because gives cause, although gives contrast, if gives condition, and when/before give time. Reread both ideas with the selected link and reject a link that changes the intended meaning.
feedback.strategy_support: Use NAME RELATIONSHIP → CHOOSE CONJUNCTION → CHECK BOTH CLAUSES → REREAD MEANING.
feedback.support_message: Chunk selection, touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC, oral composition and adult scribing are equally valid; speed, dragging, speech and handwriting are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds several adjectives without choosing details that help the reader identify or imagine the noun.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner selects repeated praise or size words and cannot explain what each detail contributes.
repair_sequence: Sort details into useful, repeated or unrelated.
Build one concise noun phrase and explain each retained detail.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner creates a fresh noun phrase with two purposeful details and rejects a redundant modifier.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats every longer group of words as a complete clause and produces a fragment after a conjunction.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner submits a because, when, if or although clause alone when the context requires a complete sentence.
repair_sequence: Find the verb in the existing clause.
Name the relationship introduced by the conjunction.
Add the missing main idea and read both parts together.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner repairs a new conjunction-led fragment and identifies both clause ideas.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Joins clauses with a conjunction that does not match the intended time, cause or contrast relationship.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The sentence is grammatically possible but expresses a different relationship from the stated meaning.
repair_sequence: Name the intended relationship in plain language.
Replay the whole sentence and select the link that preserves the intended meaning.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner chooses and explains a suitable link in a new time, cause, condition or contrast example.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds so many phrases and clauses that the main meaning becomes unclear or punctuation is lost.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner cannot identify the main message or reads an expanded sentence as disconnected chunks.
repair_sequence: Reduce the sentence to its main clause.
Return only details with a named job.
Split or repunctuate if two clear sentences communicate better.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner revises an overloaded sentence while preserving its important information.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm writing desk, stable text, muted highlights and no shaking or red failure effects.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace tile movement and bridge changes with numbered static before-and-after sentence panels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read each chunk and the complete sentence neutrally; offer slow replay and oral response before written editing.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use adjustable spacing, line focus, clause chunking, short definitions and a persistent noun-verb-conjunction glossary.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Follow a fixed FIND MAIN MESSAGE-ADD ONE DETAIL-CHECK MEANING routine with one visible decision at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, preserve the original sentence, allow undo and provide reduced choices while retaining the same grammatical relationship.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Credit concise writing, preserve correct chunks during repair and describe changes as editing decisions rather than failures.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach clause relationships with pictures and paired sentences; allow oral rehearsal and home-language discussion without assuming conjunctions map directly.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires purposeful noun expansion, complete clause linking and accurate time-or-cause meaning in at least two formats. Secure requires editing overload or ambiguity and justifying conjunction choice on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can add useful description and connect ideas with words such as when, if, because and although while keeping sentences clear.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Stores the base sentence, added noun detail, clause completeness, intended relationship and final edit separately so grammar knowledge is not hidden by spelling or handwriting.
next_actions: Route adjective piles to detail-purpose sorting.
Route conjunction fragments to main-clause reconstruction.
Apply secure expansion choices in a short paragraph without requiring every sentence to be expanded.
Schedule mixed phrase, clause and editing retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Quality-hardened Year 3 grammar-expansion pack with the same four curated proof questions and 226 deterministic pilot candidates. IDs, answers, blueprint allocation, grammatical judgements and curriculum scope remain unchanged. Every variant now has concept-specific feedback addressing noun-phrase purpose, clause completeness, conjunction meaning or editing clarity and its exact misconception. Explicit touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC/oral/adult-scribed routes, clause maps, chunking and dyslexia supports remove mandatory dragging, handwriting and speech. Missions remain untimed and penalty-free. Narration remains selectively absent; any future narration must use produced, human-reviewed ElevenLabs assets and browser TTS is prohibited. Curriculum, teacher, accessibility and safeguarding review must still verify natural alternatives, reading load and interaction routes before runtime promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
